title: Demand-Shaping Architecture

content.statement: Markets are created before they are captured.
content.question: What is the belief you need your market to hold BEFORE they'll adopt your business?
content.equation: DSA = (Education Reach × Curiosity Lift × Engagement) ÷ Time to Understanding
sop: Write the one belief a stranger needs before your product makes sense
Create one piece of content that plants that belief, not that sells the product
Measure how many people who saw it later converted
Double down on whichever format shifted belief fastest
content.explainer: If the customer doesn't already believe the category exists, you spend your first year selling education, not product. Shape demand first.
questions: What is the belief you need your market to hold BEFORE they'll adopt your business?
What would a stranger have to already believe is possible before your product even makes sense to them?
Is this month's marketing budget educating the market, or converting people who already get it?
content.equation_example: Education Reach 500, Curiosity Lift 0.40 (40%), Engagement 0.25 (25%), Time to Understanding 3 min. DSA = (500 × 0.40 × 0.25) ÷ 3 = 16.7. There's no fixed ceiling — track it weekly: a rising number means your category education is compounding; flat or falling means people still don't get it.
content.closing_statement: Teach the belief. The sale follows.

caption:
Principle Nº 2: Demand-Shaping Architecture

Markets are created before they are captured. If the customer doesn't already believe the category exists, you spend your first year selling education, not product.

Ask yourself: What is the belief you need your market to hold BEFORE they'll adopt your business?

Follow @TheGoldenSocietyClub for a new principle every day.
